CreateOAuth2Token.name: CreateOAuth2Token
CreateOAuth2Token.documentation:
CreateOAuth2Token API

Path: /v1/token Request Method: POST Content-Type: application/json or application/x-www-form-urlencoded

This API implements OAuth 2.0 flows for AWS Sign-In CLI clients, supporting both:

  1. Authorization code redemption (grant_type=authorization_code) - NOT idempotent
  2. Token refresh (grant_type=refresh_token) - Idempotent within token validity window

The operation behavior is determined by the grant_type parameter in the request body:

Authorization Code Flow (NOT Idempotent):

  • JSON or form-encoded body with client_id, grant_type=authorization_code, code, redirect_uri, code_verifier
  • Returns access_token, token_type, expires_in, refresh_token, and id_token
  • Each authorization code can only be used ONCE for security (prevents replay attacks)

Token Refresh Flow (Idempotent):

  • JSON or form-encoded body with client_id, grant_type=refresh_token, refresh_token
  • Returns access_token, token_type, expires_in, and refresh_token (no id_token)
  • Multiple calls with same refresh_token return consistent results within validity window

Authentication and authorization:

  • Confidential clients: sigv4 signing required with signin:ExchangeToken permissions
  • CLI clients (public): authn/authz skipped based on client_id & grant_type

Note: This operation cannot be marked as @idempotent because it handles both idempotent (token refresh) and non-idempotent (auth code redemption) flows in a single endpoint.

CreateOAuth2TokenWithIAM.name: CreateOAuth2TokenWithIAM
CreateOAuth2TokenWithIAM.documentation: Grants permission to exchange client credentials for an OAuth 2.0 access token scoped to a resource that can be used to access AWS services from applications

DeleteConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.name: DeleteConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration
DeleteConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.documentation: Delete console authorization configuration with automatic scope detection

DeleteResourcePermissionStatement.name: DeleteResourcePermissionStatement
DeleteResourcePermissionStatement.documentation: Remove a permission statement from the account's SignIn resource-based policy

GetConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.name: GetConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration
GetConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.documentation: Get console authorization configuration with automatic scope detection

GetResourcePolicy.name: GetResourcePolicy
GetResourcePolicy.documentation: Retrieve the account's consolidated SignIn resource-based policy

IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAM.name: IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAM
IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAM.documentation:
Grants permission to inspect the metadata and state of an OAuth 2.0 access token or refresh token

Implements RFC 7662 OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection over a SigV4-authenticated endpoint. Inspects the metadata of an access_token or refresh_token issued by AWS Sign-In and returns the claims associated with it.

Inactive token semantics (RFC 7662 §2.2): when the supplied token is unknown, expired, revoked, malformed, or owned by a different account, the response body is exactly { "active": false } with all other claims omitted.

ListResourcePermissionStatements.name: ListResourcePermissionStatements
ListResourcePermissionStatements.documentation: Retrieve all permission statements in the account's SignIn resource-based policy

PutConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.name: PutConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration
PutConsoleAuthorizationConfiguration.documentation: Enable console authorization configuration with automatic scope detection

PutResourcePermissionStatement.name: PutResourcePermissionStatement
PutResourcePermissionStatement.documentation: Create a permission statement in the account's SignIn resource-based policy

RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAM.name: RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAM
RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAM.documentation:
Grants permission to revoke an OAuth 2.0 refresh token and its associated refresh tokens

Revokes a refresh_token issued by AWS Sign-In, invalidating the entire token chain so that the refresh_token can no longer be used to mint new access_tokens.

Idempotency: revoking an already-revoked, expired, or otherwise invalid token still returns 200 OK with an empty body. Only the refresh_token type is accepted.

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating the specific type of access denial Can be TOKEN_EXPIRED, AUTHCODE_EXPIRED, USER_CREDENTIALS_CHANGED, or INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining the access denial Provides specific information about why access was denied

AccessDeniedException.documentation:
Error thrown for access denied scenarios with flexible HTTP status mapping

Runtime HTTP Status Code Mapping:

  • HTTP 401 (Unauthorized): TOKEN_EXPIRED, AUTHCODE_EXPIRED
  • HTTP 403 (Forbidden): USER_CREDENTIALS_CHANGED, INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS

The specific HTTP status code is determined at runtime based on the error enum value. Consumers should use the error field to determine the specific access denial reason.

accessKeyId.documentation: AWS access key ID for temporary credentials

secretAccessKey.documentation: AWS secret access key for temporary credentials

AccessToken.documentation:
AWS credentials structure containing temporary access credentials

Scoped, temporary AWS credentials with a 15-minute duration.

AuthorizationCode.documentation:
Authorization code received from AWS Sign-In /v1/authorize endpoint

The authorization code received from AWS Sign-In from /v1/authorize. Used in auth code redemption flow only.

BearerTokenType.documentation: Token type for access tokens. Always "Bearer" per OAuth 2.1.

ClientId.documentation:
Client identifier pattern for AWS Sign-In devtools clients

The ARN used by client as part of Sign-In onboarding. Expected values:

  • arn:aws:signin:::devtools/cross-device (for cross-device devtools login)
  • arn:aws:signin:::devtools/same-device (for same-device devtools login)

CodeVerifier.documentation:
PKCE code verifier for OAuth 2.0 security

PKCE code verifier to prove possession of the original code challenge. Used to prevent authorization code interception attacks in public clients. Must be 43-128 characters using unreserved characters [A-Z] / [a-z] / [0-9] / "-" / "." / "_" / "~"

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating conflict Will be CONFLICT

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining the conflict Provides specific information about what caused the conflict

ConflictException.documentation:
Error thrown when request conflicts with current state

HTTP Status Code: 409 Conflict

Used when the request conflicts with the current state of the resource

ConsolePermissionMaxResults.documentation: Maximum number of results for list operations

tokenInput.documentation: Flattened token operation inputs The specific operation is determined by grant_type in the request body

CreateOAuth2TokenRequest.documentation:
Input structure for CreateOAuth2Token operation

Contains flattened token operation inputs for both authorization code and refresh token flows. The operation type is determined by the grant_type parameter in the request body.

clientId.documentation: The client identifier (ARN) used during Sign-In onboarding Required for both authorization code and refresh token flows

grantType.documentation: OAuth 2.0 grant type - determines which flow is used Must be "authorization_code" or "refresh_token"

code.documentation: The authorization code received from /v1/authorize Required only when grant_type=authorization_code

redirectUri.documentation: The redirect URI that must match the original authorization request Required only when grant_type=authorization_code

codeVerifier.documentation: PKCE code verifier to prove possession of the original code challenge Required only when grant_type=authorization_code

refreshToken.documentation: The refresh token returned from auth_code redemption Required only when grant_type=refresh_token

CreateOAuth2TokenRequestBody.documentation:
Request body payload for CreateOAuth2Token operation

The operation type is determined by the grant_type parameter:

  • grant_type=authorization_code: Requires code, redirect_uri, code_verifier
  • grant_type=refresh_token: Requires refresh_token

tokenOutput.documentation: Flattened token operation outputs The specific response fields depend on the grant_type used in the request

CreateOAuth2TokenResponse.documentation:
Output structure for CreateOAuth2Token operation

Contains flattened token operation outputs for both authorization code and refresh token flows. The response content depends on the grant_type from the original request.

accessToken.documentation: Scoped-down AWS credentials (15 minute duration) Present for both authorization code redemption and token refresh

tokenType.documentation: Token type indicating this is AWS SigV4 credentials Value is "aws_sigv4" for both flows

expiresIn.documentation: Time to expiry in seconds (maximum 900) Present for both authorization code redemption and token refresh

refreshToken.documentation: Encrypted refresh token with cnf.jkt (SHA-256 thumbprint of presented jwk) Always present in responses (required for both flows)

idToken.documentation: ID token containing user identity information Present only in authorization code redemption response (grant_type=authorization_code) Not included in token refresh responses

CreateOAuth2TokenResponseBody.documentation:
Response body payload for CreateOAuth2Token operation

The response content depends on the grant_type from the request:

  • grant_type=authorization_code: Returns all fields including refresh_token and id_token
  • grant_type=refresh_token: Returns access_token, token_type, expires_in, refresh_token (no id_token)

resource.documentation: The OAuth resource for which the access token is requested. Example: "aws-mcp.amazonaws.com".

accessToken.documentation: JWT access token containing principal identity, resource scope, and session metadata

expiresIn.documentation: Token lifetime in seconds. Value is the minimum of session validity and 1 hour.

CreateOAuth2TokenWithIAMResponse.documentation:
Output structure for CreateOAuth2TokenWithIAM operation

Contains the JWT access token, token type, and expiration per RFC 6749 §5.1.

statementId.documentation: Unique identifier of the permission statement to delete

ExpiresIn.documentation:
Time to expiry in seconds

The time to expiry in seconds, for these purposes will be at most 900 (15 minutes).

GrantType.documentation:
OAuth 2.0 grant type parameter

For auth code redemption: Must be "authorization_code" For token refresh: Must be "refresh_token"

Based on client_id & grant_type, authn/authz is skipped for CLI endpoints.

IdToken.documentation:
ID token containing user identity information

Encoded JWT token containing user identity claims and authentication context. Returned only in authorization code redemption responses (grant_type=authorization_code). Contains user identity information such as ARN and other identity claims.

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating server error Will be SERVER_ERROR for internal server errors

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining the server error May include error details for debugging purposes

InternalServerException.documentation:
Error thrown when an internal server error occurs

HTTP Status Code: 500 Internal Server Error

Used for unexpected server-side errors that prevent request processing.

token.documentation: The string value of the token to introspect. May be either an access_token or a refresh_token issued by AWS Sign-In.

tokenTypeHint.documentation: Optional hint about the type of the token submitted for introspection. The server uses this hint to optimize lookup, but still falls back to the other token type on miss. Allowed values: access_token, refresh_token.

IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAMRequest.documentation:
Input structure for IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAM operation

RFC 7662 §2.1 introspection request. Contains the token to inspect and an optional hint about the token's type.

active.documentation: Indicates whether the token is currently active. true only when the token is valid, has not expired, has not been revoked, and belongs to the caller's account.

clientId.documentation: Client identifier for the OAuth 2.0 client that requested the token.

userId.documentation: User identifier matching sts:GetCallerIdentity's UserId field for the token's subject principal (e.g. "AIDAEXAMPLE" for an IAM user, or "AROAEXAMPLE:session-name" for an assumed role).

tokenType.documentation: Indicates which kind of token was introspected. One of "access_token" or "refresh_token".

exp.documentation: Token expiration time as a NumericDate (Unix epoch seconds).

iat.documentation: Token issuance time as a NumericDate (Unix epoch seconds).

nbf.documentation: Token "not before" time as a NumericDate (Unix epoch seconds).

sub.documentation: Subject of the token: the IAM principal ARN. For assumed-role sessions, this is the session ARN (matches sts:GetCallerIdentity's Arn field), e.g. arn:aws:sts::123456789012:assumed-role/MyRole/session-name.

aud.documentation: Audience of the token: the OAuth resource the token is scoped to (for example, "aws-mcp.amazonaws.com"). Omitted for refresh tokens.

iss.documentation: Issuer of the token. Always "signin.amazonaws.com" for AWS Sign-In.

accountId.documentation: 12-digit AWS account ID of the token's subject principal.

signinSession.documentation: AWS Sign-In session ARN bound to the token, of the form arn:aws:signin:{region}:{account}:session/{uuid}.

resource.documentation: The OAuth resource the token is scoped to during Human OAuth flow. Only present for refresh token introspection.

IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAMResponse.documentation:
Output structure for IntrospectOAuth2TokenWithIAM operation

RFC 7662 §2.2 introspection response. Only active is required; all other claims are omitted when the token is inactive.

IntrospectedTokenType.documentation: Introspection response token_type value. Indicates which kind of token was inspected.

IntrospectionToken.documentation: Token string submitted for introspection. May be an AWS Sign-In access_token (prefix "ASOA") or refresh_token (prefix "ASOR").

OAuth2ErrorCode.documentation:
OAuth 2.0 error codes returned by the server

Standard OAuth 2.0 error codes used in error responses to indicate the specific type of error that occurred during token operations.

OAuthAccessToken.documentation: OAuth 2.0 access token (JWT) containing principal identity and resource scope.

PolicyStatement.documentation: Individual policy statement within a resource-based policy

requestedRegion.documentation: AWS region where the VPC and VPC endpoint reside Required when sourceVpc or signinSourceVpce/consoleSourceVpce is provided

excludedPrincipal.documentation: Principal to exclude from the permission statement

statementId.documentation: Unique identifier for the created permission statement

RedirectUri.documentation:
Redirect URI for OAuth 2.0 flow validation

The same redirect URI used in the authorization request. This must match exactly what was sent in the original authorization request for security validation.

RefreshToken.documentation:
Encrypted refresh token with cnf.jkt

This is the encrypted refresh token returned from auth code redemption. The token content includes cnf.jkt (SHA-256 thumbprint of the presented jwk). Used in subsequent token refresh requests.

RequestedRegion.documentation: AWS region identifier for VPC and VPC endpoint pairing

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating resource not found Will be RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining which resource was not found Provides specific information about the missing resource

ResourceNotFoundException.documentation:
Error thrown when requested resource is not found

HTTP Status Code: 404 Not Found

Used when the specified resource does not exist

RevocationToken.documentation: Refresh token string submitted for revocation. Must be an AWS Sign-In refresh_token (prefix "ASOR").

token.documentation: The refresh_token to revoke. Must be a refresh_token issued by AWS Sign-In (prefix "ASOR"); access_tokens are not accepted for revocation.

RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAMRequest.documentation:
Input structure for RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAM operation

RFC 7009 §2.1 revocation request. Contains the refresh_token to revoke.

RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAMResponse.documentation:
Output structure for RevokeOAuth2TokenWithIAM operation

RFC 7009 §2.2 revocation response. The endpoint returns 200 OK with an empty body on success; there are no response fields.

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating service quota exceeded Will be SERVICE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining which quota was exceeded Provides specific information about the limit and current usage

ServiceQuotaExceededException.documentation:
Error thrown when service quota is exceeded

HTTP Status Code: 402 Payment Required (used as quota exceeded indicator)

Used when the request would cause a service quota to be exceeded

TokenExpiresIn.documentation: Token lifetime in seconds. Value is the minimum of session validity and 1 hour (3600 seconds).

TokenType.documentation:
Token type parameter indicating credential usage

A parameter which indicates to the client how the token must be used. Value is "aws_sigv4" (instead of typical "Bearer" for other OAuth systems) to indicate that the client must de-serialize the token and use it to generate a signature.

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating the specific type of error Will be INVALID_REQUEST for rate limiting scenarios

message.documentation: Detailed message about the rate limiting May include retry-after information or rate limit details

TooManyRequestsError.documentation:
Error thrown when rate limit is exceeded

HTTP Status Code: 429 Too Many Requests

Possible OAuth2ErrorCode values:

  • INVALID_REQUEST: Rate limiting, too many requests, abuse prevention

Possible causes:

  • Too many token requests from the same client
  • Rate limiting based on client_id or IP address
  • Abuse prevention mechanisms triggered
  • Service protection against excessive token generation

error.documentation: OAuth 2.0 error code indicating validation failure Will be INVALID_REQUEST for validation errors

message.documentation: Detailed message explaining the validation failure Provides specific information about which validation failed

ValidationException.documentation:
Error thrown when request validation fails

HTTP Status Code: 400 Bad Request

Used for request validation errors such as malformed parameters, missing required fields, or invalid parameter values.

documentation: AWS Sign-In manages authentication for AWS services. This service provides secure authentication flows for accessing AWS resources from the console and developer tools.
